comments.body: cascade = CascadeType.REMOVE? It looks like you want more then that.
@talex. in case if the record is deleted from the table I want the database automatically remove related records.

May be there is a bug in Hibernate. I have changed it to CascadeType.ALL but Hibernate automatically created the foreign key in the pictures table as NO_ACTION for both "on delete" and "on update". CascadeType.PERSIST gives the same result.

It might not be directly related to your problem, but worth a try: I think there is no need to declare the foreign key in the entity. So you should delete @Column(name = "listing_id", nullable = false) private Integer listingId;
You are right. I am updating my question.

Now it inserts if there is no nullable = false in the PictureEntity.java where private ListingEntity listing is located however listing_id is set as NULL. If I put nullable = false then I get Cannot insert the value NULL into column 'listing_id', table 'example_database.dbo.pictures'; column does not allow nulls. INSERT fails. May be there is no way to insert at the same time. I was assuming that Hibernate handles the insert in smart way.
No it definitely should work. Try out deleting all the parameters and use just @JoinColumn(name = "listing_id")

Additional explanation: Updateable and Inserteable control if the column is included in SQL UPDATE or INSERT statemens generated by the persistence provider (objectdb.com/api/java/jpa/JoinColumn ). The persistence provider is Hibernate in your case. So if they are set to false, you prohibit Hibernate from inserting data here. This leads to a NULL value, which causes the error if you set nullable=false.

answers.body:
The Listing has Cascadetype.REMOVE. This means only that if you delete your listing, all associated pictures get deleted too. For your use case you need CascadeType.PERSIST or .ALL.

    @OneToMany(mappedBy = "listing", cascade = CascadeType.ALL)
    private List<PictureEntity> pictures;

If you still have problems you should also check your DB if your foreign keys and references are defined correctly and with correct names.

Here is a simple but good introduction into the cascade types and how they interact with hibernate lifecycle:

https://medium.com/@himani.prasad016/jpa-hibernate-cascade-types-0490be19cc1f

title: Inserting records to two related tables at the same time with Hibernate
body:
I was unable to insert data into two related tables at the same time. For example I wanted to insert 5 records into the pictures table and 1 record to the listings table at the same time.

I have the sub-entity:

@Entity
@Getter
@Setter
@Table(name = "pictures")
public class PictureEntity {
    @Id
    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    private Integer id;

    @Column(columnDefinition = "VARCHAR(1000)", nullable = false)
    private String url;

    @ManyToOne(targetEntity = ListingEntity.class)
    @JoinColumn(name = "listing_id", updatable = false, insertable = false, nullable = false)
    private ListingEntity listing;
}

The main entity:

@Entity
@Getter
@Setter
@Table(name = "listings")
public class ListingEntity {
    @Id
    @GeneratedValue(strategy = GenerationType.IDENTITY)
    private Integer id;

    @Column(name = "update_date_time", nullable = true)
    @Temporal(TemporalType.TIMESTAMP)
    private Instant updateDateTime;

    @OneToMany(mappedBy = "listing", cascade = CascadeType.REMOVE)
    private List<PictureEntity> pictures;
}

When I try this

pictures.forEach(picture -> {
    PictureEntity pictureEntity = new PictureEntity();
    pictureEntity.setUrl(picture);
    pictureEntities.add(pictureEntity);
});
listingEntity.setPictures(pictureEntities);

and then listingRepository.save(listingEntity); no data is saved into the pictures table.

If I change insertable into true then I get the following error:

Column 'listing_id' is duplicated in mapping for entity 'com.example.listings.entity.PictureEntity' (use '@Column(insertable=false, updatable=false)' when mapping multiple properties to the same column

What I am doing wrong?
